payload.title: Metropolitan Transportation Authority Independent Auditor's Review Report Interim Financial Statements as of and for the Six-Month Period Ended June 30, 2024

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Established under the New York Public Authorities Law; a component unit of the State of New York
Provides subway and public bus service within the five boroughs of New York City

payload.entity_name: The Long Island Rail Road Company
payload.description: Provides passenger transportation between New York City and Long Island
Provides passenger transportation between NYC and suburban communities in Westchester, Dutchess, Putnam, Orange, and Rockland counties in NYS and New Haven and Fairfield counties in Connecticut
Operates seven toll bridges, two tunnels, and the Battery Parking Garage, all within the five boroughs of New York City
Operates certain bus routes in areas previously served by private bus operators pursuant to franchises granted by the City of New York
Provides support in budget, cash management, finance, legal, real estate, treasury, risk and insurance management, and other services to the related groups

payload.entity_name: Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority
payload.description: Provides passenger transportation on Staten Island

payload.entity_name: Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority
payload.description: Subsidiary of MTA New York City Transit, provides subway and public bus service within the five boroughs of New York City
Provides oversight for the planning, design and construction of current and future major MTA system-wide expansion projects
Provides primary insurance coverage for certain losses, some of which are reinsured, and assumes reinsurance coverage for certain other losses

tool_args.label: Governor Hochul announces pause of Central Business District Tolling Program

payload.description: Governor Hochul announced her intention to pause the implementation of the Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP)

tool_args.label: MTA Board resolution recognizing CBDTP pause

payload.description: MTA Board adopted a resolution recognizing the pause of CBDTP, extending implementation until a tolling agreement among project sponsors has been executed

tool_args.label: Central Business District Tolling Program capital funding

payload.claim_text: The Central Business District Tolling Program was established by State legislation in 2019 to manage traffic congestion in Manhattan and provide $15 billion in capital funding for the MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program

payload.claim_text: MTA Board approved a 36-month labor agreement between NYCT, MaBSTOA, MTA Bus Company and approximately 37,000 hourly operating employees represented by TWU Local 100, spanning May 16, 2023 through May 15, 2026, providing wage increases of 3.0% for 2023, 3.0% for 2024, and 3.5% for 2025 (9.8% in total), an Essential Worker cash bonus of $3,000 in the first contract year and $1,000 in the second year
